text.title: FOOD TOUR COREA in 24H
text.u: Korea we are in Seoul or should I say Soul
in September we are coming together we are
organizing the group trip with
the blueberry agency then I will put the
information below you do not have to
dress like this if you come and a question
you could ask yourself is the history perhaps
of the traditional and
ceremonial Korean unboxings or you could
ask yourself but what do we still eat
today we begin our Food tour in
Seoul from breakfast to dinner Yet
perhaps a little after dinner let's find out
starting right from breakfast let's go
the
Noble dress there was also the Prince one
Here we are are you
Ready are ready to die of
sugar and rice like this I
want you like this the shoes
We leave them here
and go
[Music]
proposed It's wonderful and they gave us
the menu now what we discover is
a typical traditional drink but I won't
tell you what yet and some sweets
stuff from companies there is not a big there
was not a big tradition of sweets in
Korea the sweets were mostly associated
with ceremonial situations or as an
accompaniment he drank more or less
alcoholic drinks But over time
the sweet tradition is developing more and more
you I'll show you something, a
lot, we ordered now, look
at least how nice all types of drinks
divided by medical drinks,
infused drinks and then here all the desserts and also
the summer section in September in Korea we'll be
back with the blueberry trip and as
many as it brings we'll all meet together
for breakfast even if in Korea the
tradition of collecting doesn't exist
for the whole history of Korea
breakfast was what you ate
even for lunch it was chemical it was rice
meat fish in that you have to die bloated or
they will hear Sauro with DNA all your own and the
stomach of titanium or I don't know how they do it
though How cute are the members of
that K-pop band those loads of
garlic omelette at nine in the
morning So everything arrived
more or less the prices are around 4-5 euros
together including the mix of desserts which the
most expensive course is 17 euros so let
's start, let's start having here we
have a tea which is chrysanthemum with
water next to it so you can play this game
here and then drink instead Here we have an
infusion of Bacchus micia which are these
berries that give a very sweet
but also acidic flavor
to the infusion and for to
fully release the sweetness they are left to
infuse even a whole night
the flavor is so astringent but
despite this so sweet
it is a skincare Spa treatment but
to your mouth Here instead we have the
pastries the Korean pastries are
all registered and a giant committee that
is Mango Which means it is not
true but it means sweet pastries and
these sweets are divided into two
large categories cuajour and toc Pikachu
are the pastries the Gramellini The tops
are the rice-based sweets I don't
know the history of all of them but
for example I can tell the
story of this and this I am still
learning eh but these here for example
are the Hua which in this
specific form are called ganjong they were
sweets that are given especially to the
rich as a gesture of greeting and they are sweets made
of glutenous rice in rice syrup
fried with a very delicate aroma
once fried they are sprinkled with
honey and
How beautiful is it it is all around armored
with dried fruit
sesame
the consistency is what you do not
expect It is really a Puff but made of rice it is
sweet the mastieri so it is very soft
with these shades of rice that
I imagine chewing cotton candy
but a moment more solid beautiful Here instead we
have I got CamSam which belongs to
that type of Korean sweets
made of dried persimmon filled with dried fruit
And it is really dried persimmon the
dried fruit filling you say okay but it's
nonsense Apart from the fact that it's delicious Don't
say these things but a type of
these the water are very important
for wedding ceremonies they were very
important for the
wedding ceremonies of the princes not only with
dried fruit they drew little
objects some animals the
butterfly wished a happy marriage
the lotus Harmony the bat luck
the Bonanno pine I
have to move sorry I have nihilite legs
and still other symbols and
still other symbols there was the pomegranate
which was fertility there was this symbol here
that was I
made it for you but like 9000 km
away and I made it for you Now let's finish
this breakfast which should
n't be called breakfast
at mass what they fry they sprinkle
mine and sesame it drives me crazy
above all listen to this sweet that
required preparation knowledge love
that a person must have been
committed to doing all these steps
to make something so small and good is
a bit like giving birth to a thread
This only tastes like good sex I was saying
let's finish everything and go for a walk
in the street because I assure you that in the
streets of Korea there is food there is so
much to eat
before I was saying Korea has not
had a great tradition of breakfast
sweets ovens also bread and apart from that it
came to them a bit over time but anyway
recipes like Joseph Hyundai these
glutinous rice balls stuffed with
red beans a bit like
Japanese images had a certain effect
when bread started arriving
in the East take those Portuguese eh
damn I until yesterday I put stuff
inside the rice balls Now I
put stuff inside the bread why not
try it and over the centuries this thing has
evolved to arrive at recipes like
Crim Punk cream
bread cream bread stuff
bread and we take one
separately Look how beautiful the little package I
took one cream and red beans
then I said Cream for creams but it's cream
the smell is that of maritozzo I
swear the smell is that of a
maritozzo
I got dirty around they can be found between
two and three euros and they are really
full of cream
too much cream it doesn't go through it is very tender the
sweet rich cream the bean that makes that
contrast Just right pulled back
You don't want anymore decided that so it
compensates a little for the delicacy of the
cream
very well let's move on The
snack break is over I'm hungry now I don't
know about you so let's go to lunch and
let's go to lunch with a splendid
Korean tradition and to try the
place I wanted to try we have to
go into a hotel
[Music]
what are you doing outside come in Stella
gets cold Have you seen how nice it is that
the Korean friends who point out
your figs because we are here for this
reason here once there were a lot and
a lot of Nomadic tribes Siberia Asia
these people were wandering around and whoever stopped them is
there with swords all violent at a
certain point these Nomadic tribes say
Listen but so much so A nice roof over your
head hot vegetables at the end of the evening
a nice pension after a long
career
we put on Ida and then they settled the
nomadic tribe that settled in Korea was
that of the Macs always a round of applause my
pronunciation otherwise
Luckily I study Korean the Macs
had a dish that was really
their dish
and what is this dish I'll tell you in a
bit because in reality First you eat
this Well I speak Korean as much as
my uncle knows about
advanced cardiology and they don't speak English here
So I can tell you what I
understood this Actually I can tell you
quite certainly it's the Don chi me you
have beautiful the Don chi me
literally means Team ci winter and it's a
kimchi broth
[Music]
then we have fried fish
fried eggplant salad various types of chemicals
sashimi and then these tagliolini which
should be like the sdraia with the
tagliolini with vegetables but I don't think I
understood well it's meat and vegetables
anyway as a seasoning for the
tagliolini dish and here the meat the meat in
Korea is very important because Why do
the Macs have something to do with it What is the dish that the
Macs cared so much about was the Mc Joe
salted meat therefore suitable to be kept
during these long travel these
crossings of these Nomads and be
consumed when you need it When
you've finished devastating the world so
what did these Nomads do that not
all Nomads were pedestrians but
anyway what did these Nomads do they
sat around a common fire and
ate this meat cooking it
together this tradition has remained
very strongly linked to
Korean culture and to this day the
Korean barbecue which is always in the center of the
table where everyone is served It's a
tradition then maybe you've surely
heard of the Albi the
Korean ribs There are a thousand recipes
a thousand different cuts of meat Now
let's see this usually together with the
original chemical meat
it was supposed to be a light lunch but
Toro lo Toro Taro loto
Thank you This is spicy and here is the
white rice The pack I was telling you I do
n't know I'm a little confused then
they try to explain to us I did
n't understand inside to explain the
history of the dishes in Korean and also
What are the dishes but I don't Damn I'm
sorry I have to improve Anyway take
sauces take salt there are a lot of
condiments
[Music]
the meat melts in your mouth You see
how tender juicy You choose the cooking
because it cooks in front of you Even
just look at the silhouette the silhouette
how attractive
it is a juicy full-bodied releases All
this flavor and yes Plus you
also add the slightly spicier flavors
like that of kimchi
its death in the end it is a cuisine that is
both heavy and light
you eat really a lot of vitamins proteins you
don't feel bloated At the end of the meal you
feel more energetic you want to
fight the war
How I like that a lot of
their dishes have the contrast cold hot there is
a dish meanwhile let's take some
fried fish there is a dish called
yum gnang which should be a
specialty especially in the north he made them for me
and he found out with the mafia Thanks Marco
that they are tagliolini immersed in an
ice soup
[Music]
the sauce I know that maybe I tell you
this and you tell me tagliolini noodles
in ice Because it goes beyond good good I
have no other words good maybe it
takes a bit of time to internalize
such a different culture in our
so structured but they
are worth it let's take some more
meat a bit of salt
now we have to finish everything This is a
godsend but if you think the day is at its
peak right now you're very wrong let's
finish everything and we'll go for
a walk first After that we'll
go for a snack
Meanwhile those in the next rooms are
cutting it up we've had
lunch and it's starting to get a bit
full we go back to the street to eat
whatever we find and actually I've already
seen the lady's stall so now I'll
tell you something
[Music]
this one takes and puts a little bit if she
gives it to me So what do we have here
First of all in Korea there's a passion
for fried food So hold the shrimp and I
assure you that eating on the street is
a delight
Look how beautiful the fried food is, very
heavy ignorant of the street but the
good shrimp is usually prepared at
home then taken to the street and
finished cooking here
instead we have cows o-en which
comes from the Japanese oderm eh
basically dough usually scraps
of white fish kartimi processing and
then anything generally from
potatoes to vegetables all in a single
dough that is spread with onion garlic
spicy stuff and the street cooks very
poor we're talking about cents Mao
But it's good, it's hot, it's soupy, and it's
really a great boost, a charge,
especially when it's cold.
But let's go to dinner, and let's go to dinner
at the market with this flavor still, to feed on the
hot broth of the beauty of the
fish, the fish heads, or if you've
noticed, in the meantime, that we're going to the market,
many of the dishes we've tasted.
Today are derivatives of other cultures
because Korea is a country that
basically gets on their nerves, they're
so beautiful here with the sea,
first China arrives, then
Japan arrives, and then it comes back for dinner, and then
the Westerners arrive. So it's
normal that many recipes have been
assimilated from other cultures and then
modified with their touch over time,
being Korean. It must really be a
real pain in the ass, but also a
great push to get to know the other. And here
we are
[Music]
it's one of the oldest and largest markets
in all of Korea, and every day
about 65,000 people pass through, some come to buy
clothes, others come to
buy costume jewelry,
spare parts, others come to eat
like us, or maybe you're asking
then. But why did you bring me here at
6:30 in the afternoon? eat
early but once you've decided
what to eat Just sit down and
order you sit wherever you want when you want
around the chef who is in the center and
serves the whole square around him and
today I've already seen
squid stuffed with vegetables our
friends from Tokyo are also
telling us their stories they're on
vacation here and so we'll take it There
it is
[Music]
[Music]
what you see instead black cut in
slices the tagliata the lady is the
baisunde it's a pork sausage with
the vegetables inside and then
added rice and basically
what you can put inside look
how she knows the cut of everything they
ask her
on the same table she cooks from squid
to pork noodles there is abalone
various molluscs this lady has the
culture of Piero Angela
Mamma mia Then everything is cooked on the
same griddle I want to see if there is any
intolerance they are fine
calm down there Here is our little plate of
stuff beautiful omelette and with hot hot vegetables it
always comes with this
plastic on top so that you don't have to
clean the plates afterwards and it's a very
beautiful and very ugly thing at the same time
beautiful for them A little ugly for the
rest because these bags are
special because it's not just plastic
on top it's actually plastic around the
plate it's placed inside a bag
Let's start eating Oh I won't even tell you
about the prices because there are no
prices I mean you really come here to eat
from Italy and with €8 you had a
wedding lunch all that's missing are the guests Here's
the bite I saw that there
really is everything I mean every single
bite you take of this thing
will fill your belly
Mamma mia the flavors and acid and Marino is
sweet and savory it's a mix of things the
squid you can tell has been here for a while
And yet you would take 27 plates
Mamma mia the time of finista Bomba that
we also go to go a little
further into the market the time behind
something else
we take the very last thing to
taste so
guang in terms of management teaches
a lot about how a market should be
at least a tourist market
because it's exactly halfway between a
tourist market and a city market So
you see the little packets of dried figs for the
tourists who want to take them
home the sweets all placed there How many in the
bags and also all the cookware that
the lady needs and the fish
Also sold directly in the tanks so
that whoever wants to do the shopping or whoever has to
cook for their restaurant comes
here and there is everything to pick up and take
home
so we Mo look the crabs
can be served both still alive and
chopped into pieces the one that was chopped into pieces was
moving even more due to the
nervous system I don't take it because these
days I've eaten so much raw food
that please stop I feel
heavy but the octopus in Korea can be
served both alive and you have to be careful
of the suckers In fact you know that many
people like to feel the suckers that
stick but it is recommended to chew
well or chopped into pieces it chopped me into
pieces recently and therefore tentacles
nervous system and everything actually
still move on the plate Clearly he
is making a face I can't describe it to you
But you can imagine we
have the mandù in front of us the Korean ravioli and I
would say that for the purposes tonight there is this what
are the mandussi now classic violoni no
from many cultures of Asia what do they
have of us special but the Mandugoreans
apart from the filling but really apart from the
filling eh stuff that some gifts
ate the Manduti pheasant a little thing
Anyway we don't know how they arrived
in Korea we described ourselves in the 17th
century 17th century that talk about it 40
are much older someone in fact
talks about Mongols in the 14th century who
arrived and found a nation dedicated
Anyway to Buddhism the meat is not
consumed but then the Mongol warrior
puts on the table this big plate of
ravioli with meat
and we are all a little less Buddhist other
theories instead trace them back to
Mesopotamia so imagine at the market it's
nice to get them mixed with meat with
vegetables with kimchi and it's also nice to
do this thing here
you understand it's a treasure look look at it
Korea is the mother of spicy the
fermented sauce the drop a little
everywhere is good Here we have a mix and
doing so you can feel even more sick
in these there was the film they are of a
unique goodness the layer of pasta is thick enough
to give both composition to the
bite but suitable not to cover the
taste of the seasoning the filling must
always have its importance Because
in the end he is the protagonist but
he looks at making a good layer of pasta is
equally important
then if you switch at the moment from the pot to the
plate There are literally a meter and
twenty with the pan behind it I'll
send you a flour another
nice thing from Mandò but I don't know if I
can show it to you It's the soup I'll send you
the book the ravioli soup you see those
bowls over there that's mandococca We
mo But we say goodbye we had a
nice day together we're finishing
dinner and it's 7.15 like but
oh well
see you in the next video I
wanted to thank this adventure in

blueberry of his information
below for the group trip for

go to Gabon Pratt I challenge you to

in love in love with a culture
as beautiful as the Korean one and Oh
